data.title: Americold stock retreats after 2025 guidance cut on demand headwinds
data.description:
Americold Realty Trust (NYSE:COLD) shares gapped down 8.5% in Thursday afternoon trading after the REIT lowered the midpoint of its full-year guidance for adjusted funds from operations, amid a challenging environment for cold storage demand. Still, its second-quarter earnings came in better than expected.

The operator of temperature-controlled warehouses now expects adjusted FFO per share of $1.39-$1.45 (midpoint $1.42 vs. $1.10 average analyst estimate, compared with $1.42-$1.52 (midpoint $1.47) in the prior view.

“Looking to the second half of the year, we expect the challenging demand environment to continue, with occupancy and throughput levels remaining below typical seasonality trends," said CEO George Chappelle.

Meanwhile, Q2 adjusted FFO of $0.36, matching the consensus, surpassing the $0.27 consensus, edged up from $0.34 in the prior quarter and slid from $0.38 a year earlier.

Revenue of $650.7M, vs. $643.7M expected, rose from $629.0M in Q1 and dipped from $661.0M in Q2 2024.

Core EBITDA was $159.1M, up from $147.6M in the earlier quarter and down from $165.5M in the year-ago period.

"Cold storage demand continues to be impacted by macro headwinds that have moderated 2H25 occupancy assumptions, which tracks with the continued weakness in the USDA inventory data, Citi analyst Craig Mailman wrote in a note.

Physical occupancy fell to 62.8% from 63.3% in Q1 and 67.8% in 2024's Q2.

Mailman added that "external growth remains a driver, but top line organic growth could remain tepid in the near term."

data.title: Long-term mortgage rates hit lowest level since April
data.description:
This week, long-term mortgage rates fell to its lowest level since April, according to the latest Freddie Mac Primary Mortgage Survey.

30-year fixed-rate mortgages averaged 6.63% as of August 7, down from 6.72% last week but higher than 6.47% in the same period a year ago.

15-year fixed-rate mortgages averaged 5.75%, compared to 5.85% in the prior week and 5.63% a year ago.

"The decline in rates increases prospective homebuyers' purchasing power and our research shows that buyers can save thousands by getting quotes from a few different lenders," said Freddie Mac's chief economist, Sam Khater.

data.title: Groupon shares rally on unexpected profit, names new CFO
data.description:
Groupon (NASDAQ:GRPN) swung to a profit in the second quarter, beating expectations and driving shares to their highest level in 4 years.

The company also announced a new CFO as Rana Kashyap will succeed Jiri Ponrt on September 1 after Ponrt becomes Groupon’s (NASDAQ:GRPN) chief operating officer.

For the quarter ended June 30, Groupon earned an unadjusted profit of $0.46, a significant improvement from a loss of $0.25 a year ago and $0.42 better than estimates. This was achieved by $31.5M in income from continuing operations and $18M in “other income” as total revenue was up by less than 1% to a slightly better-than-expected $125.7M.

Consolidated travel revenue was up 2.7% year-over-year to $5.6M, as an increase in North American travel revenue offset a 21.3% decline in revenue generated by its international segment.

Groupon had 15.8M active customers at the end of the quarter, unchanged from a year ago, with 10.8M North American active customers and 5M active international customers.

For FY25, Groupon (NASDAQ:GRPN) expects revenue to be between $497M and $505M, up 1% to 3% from a year ago and $2M better than the consensus estimate at the $501M midpoint.

Adjusted EBITDA is expected to be between $70M and $75M versus $69.3M in 2024. Groupon (GRPN) expects positive free cash flow of at least $50M for 2025.

For the third quarter, the company targets revenue growth of 2% to 5% to a range of $117M to $120M, below the $121.3M estimates.

Third quarter adjusted EBITDA is expected to be between $12M and $16M, versus $14.8M in Q3 2024. Groupon (GRPN) expects to be free cash flow negative at the end of the quarter.

data.title: European markets stay in green, BOE slashed interest rates by 25bps
data.description:
Update: The post reflects European markets closing indices.

London (UKX) -0.69% to 9,101. The Bank of England decided to cut interest rates by 25 basis points, making it the fifth cut in this cycle as the UK economy struggles with sluggish growth, persistent inflation, and rising unemployment.

Germany (DAX:IND) +1.12% to 24,193. Germany records trade surplus of €14.9B in June, missing estimaes

France (CAC:IND) +0.97% to 7,709. France trade balance deteriorates in Q2.

The pan-European Stoxx 600 (STOXX) gained 0.92% to 546.05 on Thursday, marking its fourth consecutive session of gains, while the broader market sentiment remained driven by corporate earnings and the implementation of Trump's new tariffs.

In the bond market, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was up 1 basis point to 4.24%.

Germany's 10-year yield was up 1 basis point to 2.65%.

UK's 10-year yield was down 1 basis point to 4.52%. 

Currencies: (EUR:USD) (GBP:USD) (CHF:USD)

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWG), (NYSE:GF), (NYSEARCA:EWI), (NYSEARCA:EWQ), (NASDAQ:FGM), (NASDAQ:DAX), (NYSEARCA:FLGR), (NYSEARCA:FXB), (NYSEARCA:EWU), (NASDAQ:FKU), (BATS:EWUS), (NYSEARCA:FLGB), (NYSEARCA:GREK) 

Seeking Alpha Editor Khushi Singh contributed to this article.

data.title: Cronos stock rises on second quarter revenue growth
data.description:
Shares of Cronos Group (CRON) rose on Thursday after its revenue growth in the second quarter report offset effects of a results miss.

The cannabis company posted a 20.5% rise in quarterly revenue to $33.46M, just below consensus of $33.51M. It also recorded an adjusted loss of $0.10 per share, missing analysts' estimate by $0.08 per share.

The company's sales growth was consistent with Canadian rivals SNDL (SNDL) and Aurora Cannabis (ACB) who also saw a rise in year over year revenue this quarter.

"Cronos delivered another quarter of strong performance, highlighted by record sales from Cronos Israel and continued momentum in international markets," CEO Mike Gorenstein remarked.

CRON stock is up 7% so far today. YTD, the stock has gained about 11%.

The company added that Cronos GrowCo expansion is now complete, and it expects sales from the expansion in Fall 2025.

data.title: UWM Holdings raises gain on sale margin guidance to 100–125 bps as AI-driven tech scales amid $39.7B Q2 production
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: UWM Holdings Corporation (UWMC) Q2 2025

Management View

  • Mathew R. Ishbia, Chairman, President & CEO, emphasized UWM's "strong, consistent and differentiated values" and highlighted the company's commitment to the broker channel, stating, "UWM is uniquely positioned to win in any market environment. While others have pulled back, UWM has doubled down, proving that we can and will dominate the purchase market regardless of market cycles."
  • Ishbia described several proprietary AI-powered tools, including the BOLT underwriting system, ChatUWM, LEO (loan estimate optimizer), and Mia, an AI loan officer assistant which he said is "having so much success, were scheduling meetings and helping brokers get more business."
  • UWM produced $39.7 billion in loan origination for the quarter, with $12.4 billion in refinance volume and $27.3 billion in purchase originations. Ishbia noted, "This is our third best purchase quarter of all time, and this is a big number in the market that we're in right now."
  • The gain on sale margin reached 113 basis points, a notable increase from Q1. Net income was $314.5 million, which included a $111 million decline in the fair value of mortgage servicing rights (MSRs).
  • Ishbia revealed ongoing progress toward bringing loan servicing fully in-house, targeting completion in Q1 2026, and discussed a recent partnership with Built to enhance the consumer front-end experience.
  • Rami Hasani, Executive VP & CFO, stated, "Q2 was a strong quarter for us. Net income of $314.5 million and adjusted EBITDA of $195.7 million. Production volume of $39.7 billion, up $7.3 billion from Q1 and gain margin of 113 basis points, up by 19 basis points from Q1."
  • Hasani highlighted a MSR portfolio of $211.2 billion in UPB and WAC of 5.51%, total equity of $1.7 billion, cash of $490 million, and total available liquidity of $2.2 billion.

Outlook

  • Ishbia guided for Q3 production between $33 billion and $40 billion and raised the margin guidance "up 2 levels to 100 to 125 basis points," calling it "the first time in 4 years to move the margins up to those levels."
  • He stated, "I really believe that UWM is positioned to be great in the third quarter, the fourth quarter and into 2026."

Financial Results

  • UWM reported net income of $314.5 million for Q2, with production volume reaching $39.7 billion. The gain on sale margin was 113 basis points, up by 19 basis points from the previous quarter.
  • The MSR portfolio stood at $211.2 billion in UPB. Cash at quarter end was $490 million, with total available liquidity of $2.2 billion and MSR portfolio value at $3.4 billion.
  • The company noted continued investment in technology and operational scalability, with operating expenses impacted by these initiatives.

Q&A

  • Bose Thomas George, KBW, asked about the rationale for higher gain on sale margin guidance. Ishbia responded, "we just understand the market... our margins sometimes look a lot different than everybody else's. So I feel very confident in my guidance."
  • George also queried costs related to bringing servicing in-house. Ishbia clarified, "you'll see some of those costs come out in some savings and some opportunity for us in '26."
  • Eric J. Hagen, BTIG, asked about further reducing loan closing times and the role of AI. Ishbia replied, "with the AI investments and the things we've done, it will continue to get faster... when rates do go down a little bit more... everyone else's turn times are going to get longer, and they're going to slow down more, and we won't based on our AI investments."
  • Douglas Michael Harter, UBS, questioned noninterest expense growth. Ishbia responded, "we look at the fixed expenses, that's really what we manage and monitor and understand. And a lot of them are investments... Do I think those will moderate, as you already pointed out, yes, and they will continue to moderate going forward."
  • Jeffrey David Adelson, Morgan Stanley, asked about Q3 originations guidance. Ishbia explained the range reflects "understanding the market right now with where rates were and where rates are in June and July."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts focused on guidance, cost controls, and the scalability of AI, with a generally positive but inquisitive tone, seeking clarification on new margin targets and expense trajectories.
  • Management maintained a confident and optimistic tone, with Ishbia repeatedly expressing certainty in guidance and the impact of technology investments. He used phrases like "I feel very confident in my guidance" and "we feel good about what we're doing."
  • Compared to Q1, management's tone shifted from emphasizing resilience and investment to highlighting realized growth and operational leverage. Analysts appeared more focused on understanding the drivers behind margin improvement and expense management than in the previous quarter.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Q2 production volume rose to $39.7 billion from $32.4 billion in Q1, and gain on sale margin increased to 113 basis points from 94 basis points.
  • Net income improved from a $247 million net loss in Q1 (which included a $388 million MSR fair value reduction) to $314.5 million in Q2 (including a $111 million MSR fair value decline).
  • Guidance for Q3 margin was raised to 100–125 basis points from Q2's 90–115 basis points range; production guidance for Q3 is $33–$40 billion, down from Q2's $38–$45 billion, reflecting a more cautious volume outlook but higher confidence in profitability.
  • Strategic focus shifted further toward leveraging AI-powered tools and scaling in-house servicing, with operational investments continuing but expense moderation expected.
  • Analyst attention shifted more toward profitability drivers and the impact of technology on expenses, while management communicated increased confidence in margin expansion and operational scalability.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited the costs associated with transitioning servicing in-house, but projected savings and efficiency gains in 2026 and beyond.
  • Analysts questioned sustainability of margin improvements and expense moderation, as well as the impact of AI on speed and cost structure.
  • Ishbia acknowledged ongoing investments, stating "a lot of them are investments into the scalability, into the AI, into the servicing."

Final Takeaway

UWM Holdings Corporation delivered a strong Q2 with substantial increases in production and profitability, driven by technology investments and operational scale. Management raised margin guidance for Q3 and highlighted the impact of proprietary AI tools on efficiency and broker success. The transition to in-house servicing remains on track, with anticipated financial benefits in 2026. The company projects continued leadership in the broker channel, with confidence in both near-term and longer-term market positioning supported by a robust liquidity profile and scalable platform.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Trump expected to sign order to combat political de-banking Thursday - report
data.description:
President Donald Trump plans to sign an executive order on Thursday that seeks to end practices by banks and their regulators to deny certain customers access to financial services for ideological reasons, according to a media report.

The document will order federal banking regulators to remove reputational risk standards from their guidance and training materials and identify financial institutions that have engaged in unlawful "de-banking" in the past, Bloomberg News reported, citing a senior White House official.

It will require that regulators review complaint data and refer instances of unlawful de-banking based on religion to the Department of Justice, the report said.  Financial institutions that come under the Small Business Administration will be required to make reasonable efforts to reinstate customers who were unlawfully denied services.

Trump has complained about conservatives being denied banking services. Specifically, he has accused JPMorgan Chase (JPM), of closing his company's accounts because of his politics. He also alleged that Bank of America (BAC) refused to take his accounts for the same reasons.

Banking stocks are in the red in Thursday midday trading. The KBW Nasdaq Bank Index (BKX) dipped 0.6%. Among banks by name: JPMorgan Chase (JPM) -0.8%, Bank of America (BAC) -0.6%, Citigroup (C) -0.2%, Wells Fargo (WFC) -0.4%, U.S. Bancorp (USB) -0.7%, Truist Financial (TFC) -0.6%.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.
